evidence_slots.subject: External JTAG/SimJTAG requester via tck/tms/td_i and DMI requester.
evidence_slots.operation: Read/write DMI registers and shift DMI responses out over TDO.
evidence_slots.object: DMI registers in dm_csrs and downstream debug CSRs/SBA controls.
evidence_slots.expected_guard: DMI/debug-register operations should be mediated by jtag_key/pass_chk or equivalent authorization before dmi_req_valid_o issues requests or dmi_resp data is shifted out.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: dmi_jtag_tap asserts dmi_access_o on DMIACCESS instruction and selects dmi_tdo_i onto TDO for DMIACCESS; dm_csrs hardwires dmstatus.authenticated=1 and comments 'no authentication implemented'. The dmi_cdc connects dmi_req_valid to dmi_req_valid_o without an observed pass_chk gate. jtag_key is only observed being copied to pass on reset.
evidence_slots.path: SimJTAG/external JTAG pins -> dmi_jtag_tap DMIACCESS -> dmi_jtag dmi_cdc -> dm_top/dm_csrs; response returns through dmi_resp_i/dmi_tdo to td_o.
evidence_slots.state_condition: pass_chk false/unauthenticated state; DMIACCESS selected; update_dr/shift_dr/capture_dr active; dmi_req_valid_o and dmi_resp_valid_i handshake.
evidence_slots.impact: Unauthenticated external JTAG traffic can issue DMI reads/writes and receive responses, enabling access to debug CSRs, halt control, and SBA paths.

rtl_evidence.detail: dmi_access_o defaults low and is asserted when jtag_ir_q is DMIACCESS, with no auth condition visible.
TDO mux drives dmi_tdo_i for DMIACCESS.
dmi_cdc maps jtag_dmi_valid_i to core_dmi_valid_o/dmi_req_valid_o; no pass_chk guard visible in the observed connection.
pass register is loaded from jtag_key only during reset.
dmstatus.authenticated is set to 1'b1 with comment 'no authentication implemented'.
SimJTAG pins connect to dmi_jtag and jtag_key input.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: The earlier combinational block in dmi_jtag where pass_chk may be computed was truncated; however, the observed downstream/authenticated status evidence does not show a complete pass_chk gate on DMI requests/responses.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: DMI/JTAG access appears not to be mediated by the visible JTAG key/authentication condition. The TAP selects DMI access solely from the JTAG instruction, TDO shifts DMI response data for DMIACCESS, and dm_csrs reports authenticated=1 with a comment that no authentication is implemented. dmi_jtag contains jtag_key/pass/pass_chk signals, but the observed request/response path to dmi_cdc is not shown as being guarded by pass_chk.
inspection_records.notes: High-priority candidate because the guard signal exists in the fact layer but is not evidenced on the DMI issue/response path.

evidence_slots.subject: External debugger via JTAG/DMI.
evidence_slots.operation: Halt, resume, enter debug mode, issue abstract debug commands.
evidence_slots.object: haltreq_o, resumereq_o, debug_req_o, dmcontrol/debug command state, csr_regfile debug_mode_q/dpc/dscratch.
evidence_slots.expected_guard: External debug halt/resume and debug mode entry should require authorized debug/DMI access plus legal dmactive/hart-selection state.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: DMControl write case assigns dmcontrol_d from dmi_req_i.data; haltreq_o[selected_hart] and resumereq_o[selected_hart] are directly assigned from dmcontrol_q bits. dm_top connects these outputs to dm_mem, whose debug_req_o drives core debug request. No pass_chk/jtag_key guard or unavailable/hartsel legality guard is visible on these assignments.
evidence_slots.path: JTAG/DMI -> dm_csrs DMControl/Command writes -> haltreq/resumereq/cmd_valid -> dm_top dm_mem -> debug_req_o -> csr_regfile debug_req_i/debug_mode_q.
evidence_slots.state_condition: DMControl write; selected_hart/hartsel selected; dmactive asserted/deasserted; unauthenticated/pass_chk false state.
evidence_slots.impact: An unauthenticated external debugger could halt/resume the hart or request debug entry, changing processor execution state and exposing/controling debug-mode resources.

rtl_evidence.detail: DMControl write decodes dmi_req_i.data and assigns dmcontrol_d.
haltreq_o/resumereq_o for selected_hart are driven directly from dmcontrol_q.haltreq/resumereq.
dm_mem receives haltreq_i, resumereq_i, cmd_valid_i and drives debug_req_o/cmd busy/error state.
debug_req_i causes debug_mode_d=1, set_debug_pc_o=1, and dcsr cause=request when an instruction is valid.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Full dm_mem internals are not provided, so exact debug_req_o generation is inferred from dm_top connectivity and csr_regfile behavior.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: External DMI writes can drive halt/resume/debug command state without an observed authentication guard. DMControl writes are accepted from DMI and haltreq/resumereq outputs are generated directly from dmcontrol_q for the selected hart; dm_mem then receives haltreq/resumereq/cmd_valid and produces debug_req_o.
inspection_records.notes: This finding depends on PERM-DBG-JTAG-AUTH-001 evidence that DMI itself is not authenticated.

evidence_slots.subject: External debugger/DMI requester using SBA registers.
evidence_slots.operation: Debug-initiated system bus read/write, read-on-address/data, autoincrement.
evidence_slots.object: AXI master address space reachable through dm_sba, including memory/peripherals if routed there.
evidence_slots.expected_guard: SBA should be restricted by debug authorization plus address/range/access-size policy covering sbaddress, sbdata, autoincrement, read-on-data/address.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: dm_csrs drives sbautoincrement_o, sbreadonaddr_o, sbreadondata_o, sbaccess_o, sbdata_o, and sbaddress_o from CSR state; dm_top wires these into dm_sba, which drives axi_m_req_o. The observed wiring shows no range filter or privilege/security attribute check before AXI master issuance.
evidence_slots.path: DMI -> dm_csrs SBA CSRs -> dm_top sbaddress/sbdata/control wires -> dm_sba -> axi_m_req_o / axi_m_resp_i -> SoC fabric.
evidence_slots.state_condition: sbreadonaddr/sbreadondata/sbautoincrement enabled; sbaddress/sbdata written; dmactive asserted; sbbusy/sberror states.
evidence_slots.impact: If DMI is reachable, debug SBA may read/write arbitrary bus locations, bypassing normal core privilege/PMP/MMU checks and potentially reaching protected memory or peripherals.

rtl_evidence.detail: SBA output controls and address/data are assigned from sbcs_q/sbdata_q/sbaddr_q.
dm_sba connects dmactive_i, sbaddress/control/data signals, and axi_req_o/axi_resp_i.
dm_top exposes bus master axi_m_req_o/axi_m_resp_i for system bus accesses.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: dm_sba internals and full SoC address map/routing are not provided; range-policy absence is based on visible connections and lack of guard evidence.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: Debug System Bus Access can be configured through DMI and connected to an AXI master without observed address/range/access-policy mediation. dm_csrs exposes sbaddress/sbdata/control signals, and dm_top connects them to dm_sba and axi_m_req_o; only dmactive_i is visible on dm_sba, not a protected-range policy.
inspection_records.notes: No protected-range comparator or access-control input is visible in the dm_sba port list shown.

evidence_slots.subject: DMI requester.
evidence_slots.operation: Configure/update debug command, DMControl, SBA state, and reset/debug-active state during active operations.
evidence_slots.object: dmcontrol_q, command/data/progbuf/abstractauto, sbaddr/sbdata/sbcs, response FIFO, cmdbusy/sbbusy, dmactive_o/ndmreset_o.
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Writes affecting selected hart, command/SBA, or reset/debug-active state should be blocked or error while busy; reset/active transitions should clear or invalidate stale responses and permissions.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: Data, AbstractCS, Command, AbstractAuto, and ProgBuf writes include cmdbusy checks/errors. However, DMControl writes assign dmcontrol_d unconditionally from dmi_req_i.data. The response FIFO has flush_i tied to 1'b0 and reset only by dmi_rst_ni, not by dmactive clear; dmcontrol_q deactivation only resets selected fields in sequential logic after line 517.
evidence_slots.path: DMI writes -> dm_csrs write case -> dmcontrol/command/SBA/register/FIFO state -> dm_mem or dm_sba operations.
evidence_slots.state_condition: cmdbusy_i asserted; sbbusy_i asserted; response FIFO full/empty; dmactive cleared; dmi_rst_ni reset.
evidence_slots.impact: A requester may change halt/resume/reset/active/hart selection during active operations or observe stale queued responses after debug deactivation, causing authorization/state confusion and possible stale data exposure.

rtl_evidence.detail: Data writes are blocked while cmdbusy_i.
AbstractCS and Command writes check cmdbusy_i and set CmdErrBusy in some cases.
AbstractAuto writes check cmdbusy_i.
DMControl write assigns dmcontrol_d = dmi_req_i.data without cmdbusy/sbbusy guard.
Response FIFO reset uses dmi_rst_ni and flush_i is hardwired 1'b0.
Power-on reset clears state; dmactive false triggers synchronous reset of debug-module fields beginning at line 517.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: The truncated portions around SBA register write cases and complete dmactive reset block may contain additional checks not visible here.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: Some busy-state protections exist for abstract command data/command/autoexec writes, but DMControl updates, response FIFO stale data handling, and dmactive/deactivation interactions are not fully guarded. DMControl is assigned from DMI data even while operations may be busy, and the response FIFO is reset only by dmi_rst_ni with flush_i tied low.
inspection_records.notes: Classified as candidate rather than satisfied because expected guard also covers DMControl/active transitions and stale responses, where visible evidence is weak.

evidence_slots.subject: Core software executing CSR instruction.
evidence_slots.object: Machine/supervisor/debug CSRs including mtvec, medeleg, mideleg, mie/mip, mstatus, satp, dpc, dscratch, cache-control CSRs.
evidence_slots.expected_guard: CSR operations should enforce privilege based on CSR address/current mode and raise access exceptions rather than exposing/updating protected CSRs.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: Read cases return debug, supervisor, and machine CSRs without shown privilege checks. Write cases update DCSR/DPC/DSCRATCH and machine CSR state without shown privilege checks; default alone sets update_access_exception for unrecognized CSRs. priv_lvl_o assignment also maps debug_mode_q or umode_i to machine privilege, increasing concern.
evidence_slots.path: csr_op_i/csr_addr_i -> csr_op_logic -> csr_read_write read/write cases -> CSR state/output csr_rdata_o/csr_exception_o.
evidence_slots.state_condition: Lower privilege or umode_i asserted; CSR read or csr_we true; machine/supervisor/debug CSR address selected.
evidence_slots.impact: Lower-privilege software may read or modify protected CSRs, altering trap routing, interrupt controls, debug state, cache controls, and memory-protection policy.

rtl_evidence.detail: Read decode initializes read_access_exception=0 and enters case on csr_addr.address when csr_read.
Debug, supervisor, and machine CSR read cases return register values without visible privilege guard.
Write decode executes on csr_we and updates debug CSRs directly.
Machine interrupt/vector/EPC/cause/cache CSR write cases update state directly.
priv_lvl_o is assigned machine privilege when debug_mode_q or umode_i is true.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Earlier/later code that might set csr_exception_o from CSR address privilege bits was not fully shown; no such guard was present in inspected decode snippets.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: CSR read/write decode exposes and updates machine, supervisor, and debug CSRs without visible privilege checks based on CSR address privilege bits/current mode. The read mux returns machine/supervisor/debug registers directly, and the write case updates debug/machine CSRs directly; no guard on priv_lvl_q/CSR privilege appears in the inspected decode snippets.
inspection_records.notes: The observed decode structure lacks the expected address-privilege enforcement at both read and write paths.

evidence_slots.subject: Core software executing CSR/DRET instruction; external debugger indirectly through debug state.
evidence_slots.operation: Read/write debug CSRs and exit via DRET.
evidence_slots.object: dpc_q, dscratch0_q, dscratch1_q, dcsr_q, debug_mode_q, single_step_o.
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Debug CSRs should be readable/writable only in debug mode or through authorized debug mechanisms; DRET should be legal only when debug_mode_q is true.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: Read cases return CSR_DCSR, CSR_DPC, CSR_DSCRATCH0, and CSR_DSCRATCH1 without a debug_mode_q condition. Write cases update DCSR, DPC, and DSCRATCH registers without a debug_mode_q condition. DRET operation sets dret=1 with no debug_mode_q guard; epc_o uses dpc_q if dret.
evidence_slots.path: CSR instruction -> csr_regfile read/write/dret decode -> debug CSR registers/debug return path -> epc_o/set_debug_pc_o/debug_mode_q.
evidence_slots.state_condition: debug_mode_q false vs true; csr_we true; DRET operation asserted; reset state.
evidence_slots.impact: Non-debug software can potentially read/modify debug return PC/scratch/control state or execute DRET to redirect control flow via dpc_q.

rtl_evidence.detail: DCSR/DPC/DSCRATCH read cases return values directly.
DCSR/DPC/DSCRATCH write cases update values directly.
DRET operation sets dret without checking debug_mode_q.
epc_o is set to dpc_q when dret is asserted.
Reset clears debug_mode_q and debug CSRs.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: No full illegal-instruction exception logic for DRET/Debug CSR privilege was visible in the provided snippets.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: Debug-only CSRs and DRET are not visibly guarded by debug_mode_q. DCSR/DPC/DSCRATCH reads and writes appear as ordinary CSR cases, and DRET sets dret regardless of debug_mode_q in csr_op_logic; epc_o then selects dpc_q when dret is asserted.
inspection_records.notes: Strong candidate because both debug CSR decode and DRET path lack the expected explicit debug-mode predicate in observed RTL.

evidence_slots.subject: Core software at current privilege level.
evidence_slots.operation: Configure memory translation and privilege policy via CSR writes/read and return-from-trap mode updates.
evidence_slots.object: satp_ppn_o, asid_o, en_translation_o, en_ld_st_translation_o, sum_o, mxr_o, tvm_o, tw_o, tsr_o, mprv/mstatus state.
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Only sufficiently privileged software should configure satp and mstatus privilege-policy fields; lower-privilege attempts should raise exceptions and mode transitions should update effective permissions safely.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: CSR_SATP is returned by the read mux; mstatus-derived outputs directly drive sum_o/mxr_o/tvm_o/tw_o/tsr_o and translation enable. The inspected CSR write decode does not show privilege checks before protected CSR updates, and priv_lvl_o is forced to machine when umode_i is asserted.
evidence_slots.path: CSR instruction -> csr_regfile decode/state update -> satp_q/mstatus_q -> MMU/access-policy outputs.
evidence_slots.state_condition: umode_i/lower privilege; satp write/read; mstatus SUM/MXR/TVM/TW/TSR/MPRV changes; mret/sret transitions; reset defaults.
evidence_slots.impact: Lower-privilege code may disable/alter translation or weaken memory access policy, bypassing intended memory protection and isolation.

rtl_evidence.detail: Supervisor CSR read cases include SSTATUS/SIE/SIP/STVEC/.../SATP without shown privilege guard.
SSTATUS writes modify mstatus fields using a write mask.
MMU/access-policy outputs are directly assigned from satp_q and mstatus_q fields.
mprv derives from mstatus_q unless debug mode disables it via dcsr.mprven.
priv_lvl_o assignment treats debug_mode_q or umode_i as machine privilege.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Specific SATP write case and complete privilege exception logic were not included in the provided observations.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: Memory translation and access-policy CSRs appear to be writable/readable through the same unguarded CSR decode, and outputs directly drive MMU policy. The inspected snippets show SATP readable and mstatus/machine policy fields output directly, without visible lower-privilege denial.
inspection_records.notes: Candidate based on absence of observed privilege gating and direct policy outputs.

evidence_slots.subject: Core software executing CSR or trap-return instruction.
evidence_slots.operation: Configure trap/interrupt routing and exception return targets; execute mret/sret.
evidence_slots.object: mtvec_q, stvec_q, medeleg_q, mideleg_q, mie_q, mip_q, mepc_q, sepc_q, mcause_q, scause_q, trap_vector_base_o, epc_o.
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Writes to trap vectors, delegation, interrupt enable/pending, EPC/cause registers should be restricted to appropriate privilege level.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: Read cases return supervisor and machine trap/interrupt CSRs. Write cases for MIE, MTVEC, MSCRATCH, MEPC, MCAUSE, MTVAL, MIP update state directly in the inspected snippet. mret/sret are set by csr_op_logic without visible privilege checks; epc_o selects sepc_q for sret and mepc_q by default.
evidence_slots.path: CSR/trap-return instruction -> csr_regfile read/write and exception_ctrl -> trap_vector_base_o/epc_o/interrupt state.
evidence_slots.state_condition: umode_i/lower privilege; mret/sret; interrupt_global_enable; exception input; reset mtvec load.
evidence_slots.impact: Unprivileged or insufficiently privileged software could redirect traps/returns or alter interrupt routing, enabling control-flow hijack or privilege-transition manipulation.

rtl_evidence.detail: Supervisor and machine trap/interrupt CSR read cases return values.
MIE, MTVEC, MSCRATCH, MEPC, MCAUSE, MTVAL, MIP write cases update state.
SRET/MRET operations set sret/mret without visible privilege guard.
epc_o selects mepc_q, sepc_q on sret, or dpc_q on dret.
trap_vector_base_o uses mtvec/stvec mode bits and interrupt cause.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Complete exception_ctrl and csr_exception_o generation are truncated; no explicit privilege denial was observed in the provided snippets.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: Trap vector, delegation, interrupt, EPC, and cause CSRs are exposed by CSR read/write decode without visible privilege enforcement. Machine trap/interrupt CSR write cases update state directly, and trap_vector_base_o/epc_o are driven from those registers and return-operation state.
inspection_records.notes: Medium-priority candidate aligned with the broader CSR privilege decode issue.

evidence_slots.subject: SoC AXI/APB bus master.
evidence_slots.object: ROM2 key storage/pkey_loc/jtag_key-related values, AES APB registers, packet APB registers, PLIC configuration.
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Security-sensitive MMIO should be mediated by address decode plus privilege/security attributes; secret reads and untrusted writes should be blocked or return safe values.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: ariane_peripherals instantiates AXI/APB bridges and ROM2_FUSE wiring. ROM2 copies key constants to secure_reg on reset, allows writes to secure_reg when req_i && we_i, and returns secure_reg data on reads. No visible privilege/security/protection check accompanies rom2_req/rom2_we or APB bridge access signals in the inspected snippets.
evidence_slots.path: SoC bus fabric -> ariane_peripherals AXI/APB bridge or axi2rom2 -> ROM2/AES/packet/PLIC peripheral register interfaces -> rdata/prdata outputs.
evidence_slots.state_condition: APB psel/penable/pwrite; AXI request to ROM2; rom2_req/rom2_we; reset values; read-data mux selection.
evidence_slots.impact: Any bus master able to reach these MMIO regions may read secret/key material or overwrite security policy/key state, including JTAG and AES-related values.

rtl_evidence.detail: PLIC connects to external_bus_io reg_bus.
ROM2_FUSE peripheral defines rom2_req/addr/we and connects AXI slave to axi2mem.
mem constants include access-control, JTAG, and AES key/commented values.
secure_reg initialized from mem on reset, read address latched on read, and secure_reg entry overwritten on write.
rdata_o returns secure_reg[raddr_q] when in range.
packet APB signals visible.
AES APB signals visible.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Full ariane_peripherals address decode and any bus-level privilege attribute use are truncated; AES/packet internal register policies are not provided.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: Security-sensitive peripheral/key storage paths are exposed through AXI/APB wrappers without visible privilege/security-attribute mediation. ROM2/FUSE has AXI-to-memory wiring with read/write request signals, and APB bridges expose PLIC, packet, AES, and UART buses; the observed ROM2 implementation permits reads and writes of key-containing secure_reg entries.
inspection_records.notes: The ROM2 evidence alone supports a protected-register/key exposure candidate.

evidence_slots.subject: SoC bus master and JTAG authenticator.
evidence_slots.operation: Read/write ROM2/key-related registers and use key for JTAG auth.
evidence_slots.object: ROM2 secure_reg contents, pkey_loc_o/key_reg_out, jtag_key input to dmi_jtag.
evidence_slots.expected_guard: ROM/key material should be read-only or tightly controlled; secret reads should be blocked; jtag_key should not be controllable by untrusted bus writes.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: ROM2 secure_reg is initialized from constant mem on reset but is overwritten with wdata_i when req_i && we_i. rdata_o returns secure_reg contents. The mem includes a JTAG-labeled entry. ariane_peripherals defines jtag_key output and ROM2_FUSE wiring; dmi_jtag receives jtag_key and loads pass from jtag_key on reset.
evidence_slots.path: AXI bus -> ariane_peripherals axi2rom2 -> rom2 req/we/addr/wdata/rdata/secure_reg -> jtag_key/pkey-related wiring -> dmi_jtag pass/jtag auth state.
evidence_slots.state_condition: rom2_we asserted; req_i asserted; reset initialization; bus address decode selecting ROM2; dmi_jtag reset loads jtag_key/pass.
evidence_slots.impact: A bus master may disclose or modify JTAG/AES/access-control key material and potentially control future JTAG authentication behavior or security policy.

rtl_evidence.detail: ROM2 mem constants include access-control entries, a JTAG entry, and an AES entry.
secure_reg initialized on reset and overwritten on req_i && we_i.
rdata_o exposes secure_reg contents by address.
ROM2 request/address/write-enable/read/write data and pkey/key outputs are declared.
jtag_key is wired into dmi_jtag.
pass is loaded from jtag_key on reset.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: The exact ariane_peripherals assignment from ROM2 secure_reg to jtag_key/pkey_loc_o is not fully shown in the provided observations.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: ROM2, described as holding keys, is writable and readable through ordinary request/write-enable signals, and includes a JTAG key entry. The JTAG key signal is wired from peripherals into dmi_jtag; no lock, read suppression, or privilege guard is visible on ROM2 write/read paths.
inspection_records.notes: Even if jtag_key is public policy rather than secret, the writable ROM2 path can undermine authentication/configuration integrity.

evidence_slots.subject: Test interface/testmode signal source.
evidence_slots.object: JTAG TAP clocking, DMI/JTAG access and TDO path, debug reset/active state.
evidence_slots.expected_guard: testmode_i should not bypass authentication, force DMI access, expose TDO data, or alter debug reset/clocking in a way that grants debug access in normal operation.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: In the inspected TAP logic, testmode_i is used as the select for a DFT clock mux choosing inverted vs non-inverted TCK. DMI access selection remains based on the JTAG instruction decode, and TDO selection remains based on shift_ir/jtag_ir_q. No observed testmode_i condition forces DMIACCESS, dmi_access_o, dmi_tdo_i, tdo_oe_o, DMI valid, or dmactive.
evidence_slots.path: testmode_i -> dmi_jtag_tap pulp_clock_mux2 clock selection; dmi_jtag/dm_top/dm_csrs testmode_i passed to submodules/FIFO as testmode, with no observed access enable bypass.
evidence_slots.state_condition: testmode_i asserted/deasserted; TAP shift/capture/update; trst_ni/rst_ni/dmi_rst_ni reset; pass_chk false.
evidence_slots.impact: No independent permission bypass impact was established for testmode_i in the provided evidence; testmode appears limited to clock/test plumbing in inspected RTL.

rtl_evidence.detail: dmi_access_o is selected by jtag_ir_q DMIACCESS, not by testmode_i.
TDO mux selects IR/IDCODE/DTMCSR/DMIACCESS/bypass by TAP state/instruction, not by testmode_i.
testmode_i selects the DFT TCK mux between tck_ni and tck_i.
testmode_i is passed into fifo_v2 testmode_i; no FIFO access-control bypass visible.
test_en drives dmi_jtag testmode_i while JTAG pins still drive TAP.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Lifecycle/test authorization source for test_en is not shown, and other submodule uses of testmode_i beyond the provided snippets are not exhaustively inspected.
inspection_records.notes: The missing impact slot request is closed negatively: available evidence does not show security-sensitive bypass impact from testmode_i itself.

evidence_slots.subject: Reset controller or external JTAG reset source.
evidence_slots.object: pass/auth state, DMI request/response state, dmactive/ndmreset, sbaddr/sbdata, debug_mode_q, CSR privilege state, ROM/key outputs.
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Reset should place permission/security state in safe defaults: debug inactive unless authorized, no stale DMI/SBA data, CSR privilege safe, key/auth not permissive.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: JTAG TAP state resets on trst_ni and csr/debug state reset clears many registers. csr_regfile resets debug_mode_q=0 and privilege to machine. dm_csrs PoR clears dmcontrol/sbaddr/sbdata/data and sets havereset. However dmstatus.authenticated is always 1, response FIFO flush_i is tied low and only reset by dmi_rst_ni, and dmi_jtag pass is loaded from jtag_key only during reset, making reset/key timing relevant. ROM2 restores constants on reset but permits normal writes afterward.
evidence_slots.path: rst_ni/trst_ni/dmi_rst_ni -> dmi_jtag TAP/pass state, dm_csrs/FIFO/debug state, csr_regfile privilege/debug state, ROM2 key state.
evidence_slots.state_condition: rst_ni low/high; trst_ni low/high; dmi_rst_ni low/high; dmactive_o cleared; response queue after reset; debug_mode_q after reset; ROM reset.
evidence_slots.impact: Reset sequencing or deactivation may leave debug logically authenticated, retain queued response data until dmi reset, or restore/permit mutable key material, enabling transient/stale unauthorized debug access or data exposure.

rtl_evidence.detail: trst_ni resets dr_q/state_q/address_q/data_q/error_q.
pass is loaded from jtag_key when rst_ni is low.
dmstatus.authenticated is hardwired to 1.
response FIFO reset is dmi_rst_ni; flush_i is tied 0.
PoR clears dmcontrol, command/data, sbcs/sbaddr/sbdata and sets havereset.
reset sets priv_lvl_q to machine and clears debug_mode_q/dcsr/dpc/dscratch.
reset restores secure_reg from mem; normal req_i && we_i overwrites secure_reg.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Complete dmi_reset_o/dmi_rst_ni propagation and full dmactive clear behavior are truncated; exact stale-response exploitability is not fully proven.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: Several reset defaults are safe, but reset/authentication interactions have concerning behavior: dmi_jtag loads pass from jtag_key only while rst_ni is low, dm_csrs response FIFO is not flushed except by dmi_rst_ni, and dmstatus is hardwired authenticated=1. ROM2 key registers are restored on reset but are writable during normal operation.
inspection_records.notes: Candidate focuses on reset/auth permissiveness and stale data clearing rather than absence of all reset initialization.

5: module rom2 (

14: );
15:     localparam int RomSize = 4;
16: 
17: // Store key values here. // Replication of fuse. 
18:     const logic [RomSize-1:0][191:0] mem = {
19:         192'h00000000_00000000_00000000_00000000_0000f8f8_ff6fe00f, // 3rd location for Access control master 1. First 4 bits for peripheral 0, next 4 for p1 and so on.
20:         192'h00000000_00000000_00000000_00000000_0000fff8_ff6ff00f, // 2nd location for Access control master 0. First 4 bits for peripheral 0, next 4 for p1 and so on.
21:         192'h2b7e1516_28aed2a6_abf71588_09cf4f3c_2b7e1516_28aed2a6, // 1st location for JTAG
22:         192'h55555555_28aed2a6_abf71588_09cf4f3c_2b7e1516_28aed2a6  // oth location for AES
23:     };
24: 
25: // Secure registers. Key values copied from fuse to these registers on reset.
26: //    logic [RomSize-1:0][191:0] secure_reg;
27: 
28:     logic [$clog2(RomSize)-1:0] raddr_q;
29: 
30: // We can read and write initial 64 bits.
31: // On reset, key values will be copied to registers.
32:     always_ff @ (posedge clk_i) begin
33:         if (~rst_ni) begin
34:             secure_reg <= mem;
35:         end else begin
36:         if(req_i) begin
37:             if (!we_i) begin
38:                 raddr_q <= addr_i[$clog2(RomSize)-1+3:3];
39:             end else begin
40:                 secure_reg[addr_i[$clog2(RomSize)-1+3:3]] <= wdata_i;
41:             end
42:         end
43:         end
44:     end
45:     // this prevents spurious Xes from propagating into
46:     // the speculative fetch stage of the core
